KSI-CNA-01.description: Configure ALL information resources to limit inbound and outbound traffic
KSI-CNA-01.justification: Validates comprehensive traffic controls across all AWS resources through multi-layered network security including ingress/egress controls, application-layer protection, and traffic monitoring

cli_commands.note: Analyze security groups for comprehensive ingress AND egress traffic controls
Check Network ACLs for subnet-level traffic filtering (defense in depth)
Validate route tables for controlled traffic routing and egress paths
Check NAT Gateways for controlled egress traffic from private subnets
Validate VPC endpoints for private service access (avoiding internet routing)
Check WAF for application-layer traffic filtering and protection
Analyze load balancers for traffic distribution and access controls
Verify VPC Flow Logs for traffic monitoring and visibility

KSI-CNA-02.description: Design systems to minimize the attack surface and minimize lateral movement if compromised
KSI-CNA-02.justification: Validates comprehensive attack surface reduction through network segmentation, workload isolation, service minimization, and lateral movement prevention across traditional and modern cloud-native architectures

cli_commands.note: Analyze subnet segmentation for attack surface isolation (public vs private)
Evaluate micro-segmentation and lateral movement prevention through security group rules
Assess compute workload exposure and placement for attack surface minimization
Check Network ACLs for subnet-level isolation and lateral movement barriers
Analyze load balancer exposure patterns (internal vs internet-facing)
Assess Lambda function isolation and VPC configuration for attack surface reduction
Check database placement and exposure (should be in private subnets)

KSI-CNA-03.description: Use logical networking for traffic flow controls
KSI-CNA-03.justification: Validates comprehensive logical networking through software-defined routing, traffic steering, network policies, and modern cloud networking patterns for intentional traffic flow control

cli_commands.note: Analyze route tables for custom traffic flow controls and logical routing patterns
Evaluate Network ACLs for subnet-level traffic filtering and flow control policies
Check VPC endpoints for private service routing and traffic flow optimization
Validate Transit Gateway for centralized network routing and cross-VPC traffic control
Check VPC peering for controlled cross-network communication patterns
Analyze load balancers for application-layer traffic distribution and flow control
Check DNS-based traffic routing and resolver controls for logical networking
Validate NAT Gateways for controlled egress traffic flow from private networks
Verify VPC Flow Logs for traffic flow monitoring and control validation

KSI-CNA-04.justification: Validates immutable infrastructure through Terraform-managed resources, versioned AMI pipelines, standardized deployment patterns, and replace-not-modify principles for traditional VM-based workloads

cli_commands.note: Analyze instance patterns for Terraform-managed immutable deployments (consistent tagging, recent launches)
Check launch templates for standardized, versioned instance deployment patterns
Validate Auto Scaling Groups for instance replacement patterns (immutable scaling)
Check custom AMIs for versioned machine image pipeline (immutable base images)
Validate serverless functions (immutable compute even in VM-focused architectures)
Detect Terraform state management patterns (S3 backend indicates mature IaC)
Check for Terraform state locking tables (DynamoDB indicates advanced IaC patterns)
Check for CI/CD build projects (AMI pipelines, Terraform automation)
Validate Systems Manager parameters for configuration management and versioning

KSI-CNA-05.justification: Validates comprehensive multi-layer DDoS protection through network-layer shields, application-layer filtering, edge protection, capacity-based mitigation, and automated response capabilities

cli_commands.note: Check AWS Shield Advanced subscription for premium DDoS protection and response team
Validate WAF for application-layer DDoS protection and rate limiting
Check CloudFront WAF for edge-based application protection
Verify CloudFront distributions for edge-based DDoS protection and traffic distribution
Check load balancers for traffic distribution and DDoS mitigation capabilities
Validate Auto Scaling Groups for capacity-based DDoS mitigation
Check Route 53 for DNS-layer protection and traffic routing capabilities
Verify CloudWatch alarms for DDoS detection and automated response

KSI-CNA-06.description: Design for high availability and recovery
KSI-CNA-06.justification: Validates comprehensive high availability through multi-AZ deployments, redundant infrastructure, automated failover, backup strategies, and disaster recovery capabilities across all service layers

cli_commands.note: Analyze subnet distribution across availability zones for network-level HA

KSI-IAM-02.description: Use secure passwordless methods for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise enforce strong passwords with MFA

cli_commands.note: Check for federated authentication (passwordless method)

KSI-IAM-03.description: Enforce appropriately secure authentication methods for non-user accounts and services
KSI-IAM-03.justification: Validates service accounts use appropriate authentication methods (roles, not long-term keys) and detects insecure patterns

cli_commands.note: Check IAM roles for service authentication (primary secure method)
Identify potential service users who should be using roles instead
Detect long-term access keys that may indicate insecure service authentication
Validate EC2 instances use instance profiles for secure service authentication

KSI-IAM-04.description: Use a least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time security authorization model for all user and non-user accounts and services
KSI-IAM-04.justification: Validates comprehensive authorization through IAM Identity Center permission sets (modern approach) and traditional IAM patterns for complete coverage of least privilege, role-based, attribute-based, and just-in-time access

cli_commands.note: Check for AWS IAM Identity Center (modern permission set approach)

cli_commands.command: INSTANCE_ARN=$(aws sso-admin list-instances --query 'Instances[0].InstanceArn' --output text 2>/dev/null) && aws sso-admin list-permission-sets --instance-arn "$INSTANCE_ARN" --output json 2>/dev/null || echo '{"PermissionSets":[]}'
cli_commands.note: ROBUST: Get instance ARN first, then list permission sets with proper error handling
Analyze traditional IAM roles for service accounts and legacy access patterns
Check if users have direct policy attachments (anti-pattern for least privilege)
Validate current session type (permission set session vs traditional credentials)
Get account-wide IAM usage patterns for authorization model assessment

cli_commands.note: Check IAM Identity Center for modern zero trust user access patterns

cli_commands.command: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name $(aws cloudtrail describe-trails --query 'trailList[0].Name' --output text) --output json 2>/dev/null || echo '{"IsLogging":false}'
cli_commands.note: Verify CloudTrail is actively logging (zero trust requires continuous monitoring)

Validate secure private communications (VPC endpoints for AWS services)

Validate current session type (temporary credentials indicate zero trust access)

KSI-TPR-01.justification: Validates third-party resource identification through AWS services and documented third-party inventory

cli_commands.note: Check for cross-account roles indicating third-party integrations
Check evidence_v2/KSI-TPR-01/ for third_party_inventory.xlsx, saas_services_list.pdf, and vendor_registry.pdf

KSI-TPR-02.description: Regularly confirm services handling federal information are FedRAMP authorized and securely configured
KSI-TPR-02.justification: Validates service inventory, FedRAMP authorization verification, and secure configuration compliance for services handling federal data

cli_commands.note: Primary validation through documentation: federal_data_service_inventory.xlsx, fedramp_authorization_verification.xlsx, service_security_configurations.pdf, regular_verification_process.pdf

KSI-TPR-03.description: Identify and prioritize mitigation of potential supply chain risks
KSI-TPR-03.justification: Manual evidence required - supply chain risk assessment and mitigation planning

cli_commands.note: Check evidence_v2/KSI-TPR-03/ for supply_chain_risk_assessment.pdf, risk_mitigation_plan.pdf, and vendor_risk_matrix.xlsx

KSI-TPR-04.description: Monitor third party software for upstream vulnerabilities with contractual notification or active monitoring
KSI-TPR-04.justification: Validates vulnerability monitoring through Inspector, Security Hub, and contractual requirements documentation

cli_commands.note: Check Inspector findings for third-party component vulnerabilities
Check evidence_v2/KSI-TPR-04/ for vulnerability_monitoring_contracts.pdf, upstream_vulnerability_procedures.pdf, and third_party_notification_agreements.pdf

KSI-CED-01.description: Ensure all employees receive security awareness training
KSI-CED-01.justification: Manual evidence required - security awareness training programs, completion records, and training materials

cli_commands.note: Check evidence_v2/KSI-CED-01/ for security_awareness_training_program.pdf, employee_training_records.xlsx, training_completion_certificates.pdf, and annual_training_schedule.pdf

KSI-CED-02.description: Require role-specific training for high risk roles, including at least roles with privileged access
KSI-CED-02.justification: Manual evidence required - role-specific training curricula, privileged access training records, and specialized cybersecurity training documentation

cli_commands.note: Check evidence_v2/KSI-CED-02/ for privileged_access_training.pdf, role_specific_training_matrix.xlsx, admin_security_training_records.pdf, and specialized_cybersecurity_curriculum.pdf

KSI-RPL-01.description: Define Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO)
KSI-RPL-01.justification: Validates documented RTO/RPO definitions with technical infrastructure capability verification through RDS backup retention and point-in-time recovery settings

cli_commands.note: Validate RDS backup retention (BackupRetentionPeriod > 0) and point-in-time recovery capability for RPO validation
Check backup plan frequency and retention alignment with documented RTO/RPO objectives
Check evidence_v2/KSI-RPL-01/ for rto_rpo_definitions.pdf with actual infrastructure-validated objectives, business_impact_analysis.pdf, and recovery_objectives_matrix.xlsx

KSI-RPL-02.description: Develop and maintain a recovery plan that aligns with defined recovery objectives
KSI-RPL-02.justification: Validates recovery plans with technical implementation evidence through backup configurations and recovery automation capabilities

cli_commands.note: Verify RDS recovery configuration aligns with documented recovery procedures
Validate backup plan configuration supports documented recovery procedures
Check evidence_v2/KSI-RPL-02/ for disaster_recovery_plan.pdf (updated with technical validation), incident_response_plan.pdf, recovery_procedures_playbook.pdf, and business_continuity_plan.pdf

KSI-RPL-03.description: Perform system backups aligned with recovery objectives
KSI-RPL-03.justification: Validates comprehensive backup implementation through AWS Backup, EBS snapshots, and RDS backups with retention policies and operational evidence

cli_commands.note: Check AWS Backup plans for systematic backup implementation

cli_commands.command: aws backup get-backup-plan --backup-plan-id $(aws backup list-backup-plans --query 'BackupPlansList[0].BackupPlanId' --output text) --output json
cli_commands.note: Get detailed backup plan configuration including retention policies
Validate recent successful backup operations and timing to prove backups are functioning
Confirm RDS automated backup configuration and data protection settings
Validate EBS snapshots for system backup coverage

KSI-RPL-04.description: Regularly test the capability to recover from incidents and contingencies
KSI-RPL-04.justification: Validates recovery testing through historical restore operations, backup job performance metrics, and documented test procedures with actual results

cli_commands.note: Document historical restore operations proving actual recovery testing has been performed
Show recent backup completions with timing data to validate recovery time objectives
Verify point-in-time recovery capability and backup timing for recovery testing validation
Check evidence_v2/KSI-RPL-04/ for recovery_testing_procedures.pdf, disaster_recovery_test_results.xlsx with actual restore times, tabletop_exercise_reports.pdf, and recovery_lessons_learned.pdf

KSI-INR-01.description: Report incidents according to FedRAMP requirements and cloud service provider policies
KSI-INR-01.justification: Manual evidence required - incident reporting procedures, FedRAMP compliance documentation, and reporting policy alignment

cli_commands.note: Check evidence_v2/KSI-INR-01/ for incident_reporting_procedures.pdf, fedramp_incident_reporting_policy.pdf, incident_notification_templates.pdf, and regulatory_compliance_procedures.pdf

KSI-INR-02.description: Maintain a log of incidents and periodically review past incidents for patterns or vulnerabilities
KSI-INR-02.justification: Validates incident logging infrastructure and manual incident tracking with pattern analysis

cli_commands.note: Check for security-related log groups for incident logging infrastructure
Check evidence_v2/KSI-INR-02/ for incident_log_register.xlsx, incident_pattern_analysis.pdf, vulnerability_trend_reports.pdf, and periodic_incident_reviews.pdf

KSI-INR-03.description: Generate after action reports and regularly incorporate lessons learned into operations with automated incident tracking
KSI-INR-03.justification: Hybrid validation combining automated SecurityHub incident analysis with manual after action report documentation

cli_commands.note: Retrieve SecurityHub insights to identify available incident analysis capabilities
Get specific incident insight results for after action analysis - production account specific
Retrieve resolved security findings to demonstrate incident resolution and lessons learned
Verify security standards are being tracked for continuous improvement
Check evidence_v2/KSI-INR-03/ for after_action_reports.pdf, lessons_learned_database.xlsx, operational_improvements_tracking.pdf, and incident_response_improvements.pdf
